Article Title: Call all-party meet to discuss delimitation bill: Kharge to PM Modi
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Amid reports that govt is looking to reintroduce delimitation bill in the Monsoon session, Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge Thursday wrote to PM Narendra Modi , urging him to call an all-party meeting to discuss the revised Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill.Kharge wrote that he had repeatedly asked the parliamentary affairs minister for an all-party meeting during the Budget session, but his demand was not heeded, after which the delimitation bill fell in Lok Sabha on April 17 — a reference to the defeat of the govt bill which the opposition termed as being in name for women’s reservation but having the real objective of delimiting Lok Sabha seats on the basis of population to advantage BJP and hurt the southern states.Kharge wrote to Modi that he has been reading media reports about govt move to reintroduce the bill with some changes, urging the PM that he should share the details with the opposition and give it adequate time to study the proposal before it is introduced in Parliament.Kharge’s initiative to settle the heightened speculation over the bill came even as Congress said that despite splitting TMC and Shiv Sena, Modi govt is far from achieving the 2/3rd majority required to pass a constitutional amendment bill.AICC spokesman Jairam Ramesh said Kharge, Rahul Gandhi, and the Congress leadership are in touch with all the parties “that voted against the said bill in April”, and discounted the possibility of DMK and NCP voting in its favour on reintroduction.“The home minister is trying his best to break the parties to achieve the majority mark, but he is far from it. He is attempting to achieve a tainted majority by splitting parties, which is against the people’s mandate and the constitution,” he said.About DMK with which Congress had a bitter falling out, Ramesh said Congress is in touch with the Tamil Nadu party. “They are fundamentally opposed to the ideology of BJP and the way delimitation has been done in Assam and J&K,” Ramesh said, including SP in his remarks.
Article Summary: Mallikarjun Kharge writes to PM Narendra Modi urging him to call an all-party meeting to discuss the revised Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill. Kharge wrote that he had repeatedly asked the parliamentary affairs minister for an all party meeting during the Budget session, but his demand was not heeded.

Article Title: 'Everyone does naarebaazi in democracy but ... ': Why Supreme Court told Chandigarh admin to 'argue on merits' in Bhagwant Mann case
Article Text:
Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann (File photo)

(With PTI inputs)

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Thursday told the Chandigarh administration to "argue on merits" in a plea linked to Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann , saying it was otherwise "not inclined to interfere" in the matter.A bench headed by Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant made the observation while hearing the Chandigarh administration's appeal against a Punjab and Haryana high court order quashing an FIR against Mann — then a Lok Sabha MP — and other AAP leaders over a 2020 protest against a hike in electricity tariffs."Everyone does 'naarebaazi' (sloganeering) in a democracy. Now that he (Mann) is holding some responsible position, we understand that he will understand his responsibility also," the CJI said."If you are going to argue on merits, we will hear you. Otherwise, we are not inclined to interfere," the CJI told Additional Solicitor General SV Raju, appearing for the Chandigarh administration, after the senior counsel sought time to put the judicial records in order.Raju submitted that the Chandigarh administration was facing certain technical objections in three separate petitions challenging different high court orders and sought time to address the issue.The high court had earlier held that no prima facie case was made out against the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) leaders and that the ingredients of the offences invoked under the Indian Penal Code (IPC) were absent.It consequently quashed the FIR and the chargesheet registered under Sections 147 (rioting), 149 (unlawful assembly), 332 (voluntarily causing hurt to deter a public servant from duty) and 353 (assault or criminal force to deter a public servant from discharging duty) of the IPC.The FIR was registered on the complaint of constable Manpreet Kaur, who alleged that during an AAP protest against the hike in electricity tariffs, senior party leaders, including Mann, Harpal Singh Cheema, Gurmeet Singh Meet Hayer, Baljinder Kaur and Aman Arora, incited around 750-800 party workers to march towards then Punjab chief minister's residence in Sector 2, Chandigarh.According to the police, the protesters attempted to breach barricades and later resorted to stone-pelting after water cannons were used to disperse the gathering, leaving several police personnel with simple injuries.
Article Summary: The Supreme Court on Thursday told the Chandigarh administration to "argue on merits" in a plea linked to Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann. A bench headed by Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant made the observation while hearing the appeal against a Punjab and Haryana high court order quashing an FIR against Mann.

Article Title: 'Brazenly working like a BJP branch office': Derek O'Brien slams ECI over extensions to rebel TMC faction
Article Text:
TMC MPs Sagarika Ghose, Derek O'Brien, and Saket Gokhale

(With ANI inputs)

NEW DELHI: Derek O'Brien, Rajya Sabha MP of the Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress (TMC) faction, on Thursday accused the Election Commission (ECI) of "working like a branch office" of the BJP His remarks came over the extensions granted by the poll body to the rebel TMC faction in the dispute over which side is the "real" Trinamool Congress."We responded to the Election Commission within the laid-down deadline of just three days. On the other hand, the EC has already granted two unprecedented extensions to the new Traitor Party for 23 days, and yet they have not been able to furnish a reply. EC should not brazenly work like a branch office of the BJP," he said at a press conference in Kolkata.On July 2, the ECI sought responses from both the Mamata Banerjee-led faction and the rebel group led by Ritabrata Banerjee after they made conflicting claims over the party's authorised organisational structure and signatories.The poll body had asked both sides to file their replies by 5:30 pm on July 6. While the Mamata Banerjee camp complied with the deadline, the Ritabrata Banerjee faction was granted an extension until 5:30 pm on July 10.The rebels were later given a second extension, this time until July 25.Earlier this week, senior Mamata Banerjee faction leader Kalyan Banerjee said the party had filed a detailed reply before the Election Commission, asserting that the tenure of the TMC's National Working Committee remains valid until 2027 under the party's constitution.The TMC split after its defeat to the BJP in the April assembly elections, ending its 15-year rule in West Bengal. Since then, more than 60 of its 80 newly elected MLAs, including several Mamata Banerjee loyalists, have joined the rebel faction.In the Lok Sabha, 20 of the TMC's 28 MPs merged with the little-known Nationalist Citizens Party of India and pledged support to the BJP-led ruling National Democratic Alliance (NDA) at the Centre.
Article Summary: Derek O'Brien, Rajya Sabha MP of the Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress (TMC) faction, accused the Election Commission (ECI) of "working like a branch office" of the BJP. His remarks came over the extensions granted by the poll body to the rebel TMC faction in the dispute over which side is the "real" TMC.

Article Title: 'Those under pressure, go wherever you want': Mamata as TMC exodus continues with Koel Mallick's exit
Article Text:
Former Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee (PTI file photo)

NEW DELHI: Former West Bengal chief minister and TMC supremo Mamata Banerjee on Thursday reacted on the wave of ongoing exodus from Trinamool Congress following the party's crushing assembly poll defeat in May. Reacting to actor-politician Koel Mallick's resignation from the Rajya Sabha, Mamata suggested that several leaders are "under pressure" and said those unwilling to stay were free to leave."I saw one MP. I respect her. She had informed earlier through email. Today she met a BJP leader. Those who are under pressure, please take whatsoever decision you want and go wherever you want to before 21st July Shaheed Diwas," news agency ANI quoted Mamata as saying.Her remarks came shortly after Koel Mallick resigned as a Rajya Sabha member, dealing another blow to the Mamata Banerjee-led party amid a string of exits by senior leaders, including MPs and MLAs."Rajya Sabha MP from TMC, Koel Mallick, met Vice President and Rajya Sabha Chairman C P Radhakrishnan and submitted her resignation," sources told ANI earlier Thursday.Mallick's resignation comes a day after TMC MLA and Mamata Banerjee's close aide Madan Mitra quit the party to join the rebel TMC faction led by Ritabrata Banerjee.On Wednesday, Mamata had also come out strongly in defence of her nephew and the party's general secretary, Abhishek Banerjee , asserting that he had "done no wrong" and would remain in politics for the next 50 years.Her remarks came as the party grappled with mounting defections and an internal rebellion over Abhishek's growing influence.
Article Summary: Mamata suggests that several leaders are "under pressure" and said those unwilling to stay were free to leave. Her remarks came shortly after Koel Mallick resigned as a Rajya Sabha member, dealing another blow to the Mamata Banerjee-led party.

Article Title: 'We'll go to Delhi and raise our voice': Omar Abdullah vows to press for J&K statehood demand
Article Text:
J&K CM Omar Abdullah (PTI file photo)

NEW DELHI: Jammu and Kashmir chief minister Omar Abdullah on Thursday said the National Conference's planned protest in Delhi on July 20 over the delay in restoring statehood to Jammu and Kashmir will go ahead as scheduled, though the venue or the format of the demonstration may change.He, however, declined to describe the delay in getting permission for the protest as sabotage or an attempt to divert attention."I will not go into whether it is a sabotage, diversion or anything else. You and I both understand it. Our programme will not change, the venue or the mode may change, but the programme to go to Delhi will not change," Abdullah told reporters.The chief minister said the party would not hold the protest discreetly or in a private space."Had that been the case, we would have held it in the lawns of our house. But we will go to Delhi and raise our voice there somehow. Then see what is to be done in the future," he added.The ruling National Conference has announced a sit-in protest at Jantar Mantar on July 20, the opening day of the Monsoon session of Parliament, to "remind" the Centre of its promise to restore statehood to Jammu and Kashmir.
Article Summary: National Conference's planned protest in Delhi on July 20 will go ahead as scheduled. Chief minister Omar Abdullah declined to describe the delay in getting permission as sabotage or an attempt to divert attention. He said the party would not hold the protest discreetly or in a private space. The ruling National Conference has announced a sit-in protest at Jantar Mantar.

Article Title: Senior BJP leader, former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passes away at 88
Article Text: BENGALURU: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88 at a private hospital in Bengaluru on Tuesday.A veteran leader, Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades.Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death of senior BJP leader and former minister Ramachandra Gowda, describing him as a "brother" and a longtime companion.In a post on X, Yediyurappa recalled their journey together since the Jana Sangh era, their efforts to build the party in Karnataka, and praised Gowda's administrative acumen and dedication as a minister.BS Yediyurappa tweets, "I have lost yet another brother. The news of the passing of Shri Ramachandra Gowda, my soulmate of decades, senior leader, and former minister, has brought me immense sorrow. The days of struggle—standing shoulder to shoulder since the Jana Sangh era, toiling day and night, building the party from the grassroots level together in the state—swirl before my eyes. As a colleague in my cabinet, I have witnessed his administrative acumen and dedication up close. "
Article Summary: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88. Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades. Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death.

Article Title: 1994-batch IPS officer Anurag Kumar replaces Satish Golcha as new Delhi Police commissioner
Article Text:
1994-batch IPS officer Anurag Kumar replaces Satish Golcha as new Delhi Police commissioner





NEW DELHI: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has appointed 1994-batch IPS officer Anurag Kumar as the new Commissioner of Police for Delhi. Kumar was currently posted as special director ( intelligence bureau ).According to an official order issued on Friday, Kumar will assume the top post in the capital's police force with effect from the date he assumes charge. He is slated to hold the position until further orders are issued.He succeeds the outgoing Commissioner, Satish Golcha, a 1992-batch IPS officer from the AGMUT cadre, who has been directed to report to the Lieutenant Governor of Delhi for a subsequent posting.This transition will take full effect upon the assumption of charge by the new incumbent, Kumar.
Article Summary: 1994-batch IPS officer Anurag Kumar replaces Satish Golcha as new Delhi Police commissioner. Kumar was currently posted as special director ( intelligence bureau) He succeeds the outgoing Commissioner, Satish. Golcha, who has been directed to report to the Lieutenant Governor of Delhi for a subsequent posting.

Article Title: AAP holds funeral procession of a bike against ethanol-blended petrol in Kolhapur
Article Text: Aam Aadmi Party members hold a symbolic funeral procession for a two-wheeler in Kolhapur on Thursday to protest the mandatory sale of ethanol-blended petrol
Article Summary: Aam Aadmi Party members hold a symbolic funeral procession for a two-wheeler in Kolhapur on Thursday to protest the mandatory sale of ethanol-blended petrol. The funeral procession was led by a member of the party's state unit. The party is demanding a ban on the use of ethanol in petrol.

Article Title: Building contractor dies by suicide, 14 booked
Article Text:
Pic for representation

Pune:





A 65-year-old building contractor was found hanging from the ceiling fan at around 8 am on Thursday in his house at Gultekdi, Market Yard.He purportedly wrote two suicide notes naming five persons, including a BJP worker, the wife of a former MLA and others, the Swargate police said.Senior inspector Yashwant Nikam of Swargate police told TOI, “He pasted these notes on the wall of his house. These notes state his financial transactions and property deals with five different persons, including a builder. After studying the notes and studying the circumstances, we can confirm that it is a case of suicide”.“In these notes, he has named a BJP worker, the wife of former MLA from city, and others,” he said.The notes claimed harassment by these people, Nikam said.The Swargate police have registered an accidental death case late on Thursday night after receiving the post-mortem report. “We will register an FIR in this regard after recording statements of the deceased’s family members”, Nikam said.Later in the night, the Swargate police registered a case of abetment to suicide against 14 people, including the BJP worker, a builder, a former MLA and his wife.
Article Summary: A 65-year-old building contractor was found hanging from the ceiling fan at around 8 am on Thursday in his house at Gultekdi, Market Yard. He purportedly wrote two suicide notes naming five persons, including a BJP worker, the wife of a former MLA and others, the Swargate police said.

Article Title: Court extends Siya Goyal, Chetan Chaudhary’s judicial custody till July 29
Article Text:
Accused in the Ketan Agarwal murder case Siya Goyal being taken to court from Vadgaon Maval police station

Pune:





A Wadgaon Maval court on Thursday extended the judicial custody of Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary till July 29.Goyal and Chaudhary were arrested for allegedly murdering Goyal’s fiancé, Ketan Agarwal, by pushing him off a cliff at Lohagad Fort near Lonavla on June 18.The Pune Rural police arrested the duo on June 23. After spending 10 days in police custody, the accused were remanded to 14 days of judicial custody.Goyal is currently lodged at Yerawada Women’s Prison, while Chaudhary is lodged at Yerawada Central Prison.Chaudhary’s lawyer, Ram Shahane, told TOI that the judicial custody of both accused ended on Thursday. “They were produced before the court through video conferencing. The court extended their judicial custody till July 29,” Shahane said.Meanwhile, a Pune Rural police team visited a temple in Rajasthan to verify whether Goyal and Chaudhary had secretly married. The police recovered data from the duo’s cellphones and suspect they may have solemnised their marriage at a temple in Rajasthan.“Our team examined CCTV footage from the temple and nearby areas, but found no evidence to support this,” an investigating officer said.The team also visited a few other locations in Rajasthan to gather information about Goyal and Chaudhary’s movements during their visit. “Our investigation is ongoing,” he said.
Article Summary: Goyal and Chaudhary were arrested for allegedly murdering Goyal’s fiancé, Ketan Agarwal, by pushing him off a cliff at Lohagad Fort near Lonavla on June 18. After spending 10 days in police custody, the accused were remanded to 14 days of judicial custody.

Article Title: Auto driver’s body found in well in Tiruvallur
Article Text:
Murder suspected

Chennai:





An autorickshaw driver who went out to consume alcohol with two friends was found dead in a farm well near Kanakammachatram in Tiruvallur district on Wednesday.The deceased was identified as Rajarathinam, 30, of Chinnammapettai near Thiruvalangadu. Police said he had gone to Kanakammachatram on a motorcycle with his friends, Nirmalkumar, 32, and Tamizhan, 31, to consume alcohol.After drinking, Nirmalkumar and Tamizhan returned home, but Rajarathinam did not. Subsequently, his family started searching for him.Meanwhile, Kanakammachatram police received information that a man’s body was floating in an agricultural well owned by Gopi at Muthukondapuram in their jurisdiction. Police rushed to the spot, recovered the body and identified it as Rajarathinam. The body was sent to the Tiruvallur Govt Medical College Hospital for postmortem.Police have registered a case and are investigating whether it was a case of murder or an accident.
Article Summary: The deceased was identified as Rajarathinam, 30, of Chinnammapettai near Thiruvalangadu. He had gone to Kanakammachatram on a motorcycle with his friends, Nirmalkumar, 32, and Tamizhan, 31.

Article Title: Pocso accused who escaped from police custody at Stanley hospital caught
Article Text:
Back in custody

Chennai:





A 21-year-old POCSO accused, who escaped from police custody while undergoing treatment at Stanley Govt Hospital on Wednesday night, was caught on Thursday.The accused, Raja of Pudur, was arrested by the Ambattur All Women Police in a POCSO case on June 15 and had been remanded to judicial custody at Puzhal prison. He was brought to Stanley Govt Hospital under armed police escort after complaining of stomach pain. While waiting near the emergency ward, he asked for water.When one constable went to a nearby canteen to buy a water bottle, Raja distracted the attention of the other policeman guarding him and fled from the hospital premises.Based on a complaint, Washermenpet police registered a case and launched a search to trace the escaped prisoner. He was traced to a shop near Villivakkam.
Article Summary: The accused, Raja of Pudur, was arrested by the Ambattur All Women Police in a POCSO case on June 15 and had been remanded to judicial custody at Puzhal prison. He was brought to Stanley Govt Hospital under armed police escort after complaining of stomach pain. While waiting near the emergency ward, he asked for water.

Article Title: Two arrested for assault and abduction of debtor
Article Text:
Busted

Chennai:





Two men were arrested on charges of abducting and assaulting a 39-year-old lodge owner in Nolambur over a ₹15lakh debt. The two had also attempted to transfer ownership of his house in lieu of the debt.The complainant, Priyadarshan of Brindavan Avenue in Nolambur, had borrowed ₹22.5lakh from Senguttuvan to construct a lodge and repaid ₹7.5lakh. On May 29, three unidentified men approached Priyadarshan near his house, forced him into a car, assaulted him and took him to Senneerkuppam.Senguttuvan demanded Priyadarshan to transfer his house in lieu of the outstanding loan. The victim was later confined in a private lodge before being dropped near the Maduravoyal Bypass. Based on his complaint, Nolambur police registered a case under kidnapping and other relevant charges.Following an investigation, police arrested Vignesh , 34, and Kamal , 33, both from Senneerkuppam. The two were remanded in judicial custody. Police have launched a search for Senguttuvan and the other suspects.
Article Summary: Two men arrested on charges of abducting and assaulting a 39-year-old lodge owner in Nolambur. The two had also attempted to transfer ownership of his house in lieu of the debt. The complainant, Priyadarshan of Brindavan Avenue, had borrowed ₹22.5lakh from Senguttuvan to construct a lodge.

Article Title: SSI held by DVAC for taking ₹70,000 bribe in Coimbatore
Article Text:
Coimbatore:





Sleuths from thedirectorate of vigilance and anti-corruption (DVAC) on Thursday arrested a special sub-inspector attached to the Kattoor police station for accepting a bribe of ₹70,000.The arrested has been identified as Boopathi. He was arrested while receiving the bribe near the Ukkadam bus stand on Thursday evening.According to DVAC officials, the complainant, Rujaina Fathima of Tenkasi, had mortgaged her husband’s off-road sports utility vehicle to Ravikumar of Tirunelveli and received ₹6 lakh from him. They had agreed that the vehicle would be returned once Fathima repaid the amount.A week ago, when Ravikumar visited Coimbatore in the vehicle. Fathima and her husband tracked the SUV using the GPS device fitted in it, came to Coimbatore and took the vehicle using a spare key. They later parked it at a parking facility in Pollachi.Ravikumar then lodged a complaint with Kattoor police and a case was registered. Police subsequently secured the vehicle and brought Fathima to the station for inquiry. They also took her mobile phones, citing investigation. Fathima subsequently settled the amount with Ravikumar and reclaimed the vehicle.However, Boopathi allegedly contacted her and demanded ₹70,000 to close the case and return her mobile phones.Unwilling to pay the bribe, Fathima approached DVAC officials on Wednesday evening, following which they handed over chemical coated currency notes to her.On Thursday evening, Fathima met Boopathi near the Ukkadam bus stand and handed over the money. DVAC sleuths, who were waiting nearby, caught Boopathi red-handed after he accepted the bribe and arrested him.DVAC officials have registered a case and are investigating further.
Article Summary: Sleuths from thedirectorate of vigilance and anti-corruption (DVAC) on Thursday arrested a special sub-inspector attached to the Kattoor police station. The arrested has been identified as Boopathi. He was arrested while receiving the bribe near the Ukkadam bus stand on Thursday evening.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Karti backs DMK-TVK cooperation at nat’l level
Article Text:
Congress





Madurai: Congress MP Karti Chidambaram has backed VCK president Thol Thirumavalavan’s suggestion that DMK and TVK could cooperate at the national level despite being rivals in Tamil Nadu.“Parties can have competing interests in a state, but when it comes to national politics—and if they are committed to secular politics—there can be cooperation,” Karti told reporters in Madurai. He cited Kerala, where Congress and CPM contest against each other in the state but work together on issues in Parliament.“But that requires a certain amount of political maturity,” he said. He also referred to West Bengal, where Congress and TMC had competed electorally but cooperated on parliamentary matters.Asked about former BJP leader K Annamalai quitting the party and launching a movement, Karti said, “Good luck to him. I mean, he has realised that the BJP will not take him very far.” He rejected suggestions that Annamalai’s movement could function as a BJP proxy in Tamil Nadu. “No, I think BJP has no team in Tamil Nadu. They don’t have A team, B team or Z team. BJP is a rejected team in Tamil Nadu,” he added.
Article Summary: Congress MP Karti Chidambaram has backed VCK president Thol Thirumavalavan’s suggestion that DMK and TVK could cooperate at the national level despite being rivals in Tamil Nadu. He cited Kerala, where Congress and CPM contest against each other in the state but work together on issues in Parliament.

Article Title: Woman found dead with her throat slit in Virudhunagar district
Article Text:
Woman found dead with her throat slit in Virudhunagar district





Madurai: A 31-year-old woman was found dead with her throat slit at her house near Kariapatti in Virudhunagar district on Thursday.Police identified the deceased as Mayil of Keela Azhagiyanallur village. She was married to Muniyasamy, a construction worker, and the couple has two children. On Thursday morning, Mayil was alone at the house after her husband left for work and the children went to school.During the evening, when her son studying Class V came home, he found his mother lying dead in a pool of blood with her throat slit. She was found holding a knife in her hand.On information, police rushed to the spot and sent her body to the Virudhunagar govt medical college hospital for postmortem. Mallankinaru police registered a case and are investigating.
Article Summary: Police identified the deceased as Mayil of Keela Azhagiyanallur village. She was married to Muniyasamy, a construction worker, and the couple has two children. Mayil was alone at the house after her husband left for work.

Article Title: Case over derogatory post on Kerala CM
Article Text:
Kerala CM V D Satheesan

Kochi

:





Puthenvelikkara police in Ernakulam have registered a case against a man following a complaint that he allegedly posted derogatory content on social media targeting chief minister V D Satheesan.The FIR was registered on Thursday based on the complaint of Anikuttan, a resident of Puthanvelikkara. The accused has been identified as Raju, also of Puthanvelikkara.The complainan alleged that Raju made a post using obscene language targeting the CM on July 11. Police registered an FIR under relevant sections of BNS, IT Act and Kerala Police Act.Police said that further action would be initiated after an enquiry.
Article Summary: Police in Ernakulam have registered a case against a man following a complaint that he allegedly posted derogatory content on social media. The accused has been identified as Raju, also of Puthanvelikkara. The complainan alleged that Raju made a post using obscene language targeting the CM on July 11.
